title: dalea pennellii var. pennellii
intro: Dalea pennellii var. pennellii, also known as the pink or white-flowering dalea, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a type of legume that belongs to the pea family and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, fever, and respiratory problems, and has been shown to have antioxidant and antispasmodic effects, which can help to reduce stress and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of dalea pennellii var. pennellii can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
